name: advanced_circuit_adjacent_cluster_breaks_ug_capped
notes: Single-crossing cluster at (22,143) — iron-ore East (y=143) crosses iron-plate South (x=22, fed from a West-flowing feeder at y=142). Should be trivially solvable with a 2-tile UG pair on either spec. Caps because the ADJACENT cluster at (25-26,142-143) is solved first and leaves an iron-plate UG pair committed at (27,142→24,142) — those tiles carry segment_id "crossing:"/"junction:" which ghost_router.rs:1150-1164 EXCLUDES from unreleasable_obstacles. When (22,143)'s region growth expands eastward past iter 2 to a 4×4 bbox covering (24,142), SAT treats it as a free tile, proposes overwriting the UG-out with a surface belt, and the walker correctly vetoes because the UG-pair invariant (27,142 UG-in ↔ 24,142 UG-out) breaks. Every variant in every iter from 2 through 4 suffers the same veto. Fix candidates: (a) include junction-stamped UG pairs in unreleasable_obstacles; (b) encode already-committed UGs as interior/forced tiles when a neighbouring SAT zone overlaps them; (c) tighten cluster merging so these two clusters solve together.
